user_query: What were the other options for father day 1 dinner before we deleted them?

selected_nodes.name: One-Pot Greek Chicken & Orzo
selected_nodes.short_description: Tender chicken thighs braised with orzo, tomatoes, olives, and artichoke hearts in a lemony broth — a comforting one-pot Mediterranean dinner.
selected_nodes.meal_suggestion: Brown seasoned chicken thighs in olive oil in a deep skillet. Remove and sauté onion and garlic, then add diced tomatoes, chicken broth, orzo, sliced kalamata olives, and canned artichoke hearts. Nestle the chicken back in, cover, and simmer 20 minutes until orzo is tender and liquid is absorbed. Finish with lemon juice, crumbled feta, and chopped fresh oregano.
selected_nodes.nutrition_note: Chicken thighs provide protein with more flavor than breast; orzo supplies carbohydrates; tomatoes and artichoke hearts are rich in fiber, vitamin C, and antioxidants; olives contribute monounsaturated fats. A balanced one-pot meal with protein, carbs, and vegetables. Use low-sodium broth to manage salt; skin-on thighs add richness but also saturated fat — remove skin if preferred.
selected_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 3 > Father > Day 1 > Dinner > One-Pot Greek Chicken & Orzo
selected_nodes.reasoning: This node represents the 'One-Pot Greek Chicken & Orzo' which was explicitly deleted from Father's Day 1 Dinner in the version context.

selected_nodes.name: Herb-Crusted Baked Sea Bass with Roasted Vegetables
selected_nodes.short_description: Flaky sea bass baked with a Provençal herb crust, served alongside roasted zucchini, eggplant, and cherry tomatoes drizzled with olive oil.
selected_nodes.meal_suggestion: Season sea bass fillets with a mixture of breadcrumbs, dried herbs de Provence, lemon zest, and a drizzle of olive oil. Bake at 400°F for 15–18 minutes. Toss zucchini, eggplant, and cherry tomatoes with olive oil, salt, and pepper; roast on a separate tray. Plate the fish over the vegetables and finish with a squeeze of lemon and fresh basil leaves.
selected_nodes.nutrition_note: Sea bass is a lean white fish high in protein and rich in selenium and B vitamins; roasted Mediterranean vegetables provide fiber, vitamins A and C, and potassium. Olive oil adds anti-inflammatory monounsaturated fats. Herbs de Provence (thyme, rosemary, oregano) contribute antioxidants. A light, elegant dinner aligned with the Mediterranean diet.
